41.reason: [Syntax error: 'else' must be followed by a statement or block, not a parenthesized expression. Should be 'else { printf(...); }' or just 'else printf(...);']

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Syntax error: 'else' must be followed by a statement or block, not a parenthesized expression. Should be 'else { printf(...); }' or just 'else printf(...);']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Syntax error: 'else' should not be followed by parentheses; should be 'else printf(...)' or 'else { ... }']
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [Syntax error: 'else' must be followed by a statement or block, not a parenthesized expression. Should be 'else { printf(...); }' or just 'else printf(...);' without parentheses around the call.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Syntax error: 'else' must be followed by a statement or block, but parentheses around printf make this invalid syntax; should be 'else printf(...);' without parentheses]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Syntax is unconventional but valid; however, more critically, when i=0 and s[0]=='-', accessing s[i-1] (i.e., s[-1]) leads to undefined behavior (buffer underflow). Similarly, if '-' is at the last character, s[i+1] is out of bounds.]

38.reason: [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if', so if any of the first two 'if' conditions are true but the third is false, it incorrectly prints '-' even when expansion should occur]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if', so if any of the first two 'if' conditions are true but the third is false, it incorrectly prints '-' even when expansion should occur]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This 'else' only pairs with the last 'if' (digit case), not the entire group. So if the '-' is between uppercase letters, it expands correctly but then also prints '-' because the digit condition fails and triggers the else. This causes duplicate or incorrect output.]
